ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.
text: Juuda jipɔŋkpiliu Eer nìn sá unikpiti ti-Dindaan nimbiin, an puee ní ti-Dindaan yāntì u kpíì.
translated: Judas, who also was called Jude, wrote a letter to the believers, urging them to contend for the faith that was once for all delivered to the saints.

ref: The king said to him, “I had a dream, yet no one can explain what it means. I am told that you can interpret dreams.”
text: U bàn̄ Faaroo ceeyee ní Faaroo bí-u yii: «M dámintì kudamintiiu kubɔ ní báà ubɔ kaa ŋúń ki tùkū-mi ku-taapu. Ama m gbìl̄ yii bi yaa kàan̄ kudamintiiu ki tùkū-see, a ŋūǹ kí lēē ku-taapu.»
translated: “I don’t want to tell you a lie, but I’m afraid of what people will say if I don’t do it. But if I do it, I’m afraid of what people will say.”

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Bin pūul̀ ubɔti kpɔnɔbee kpiliu nín ká yii Jooseefi tùkū u-bɔɔ u-damintiiku taapu an ŋān̄ee, ní u mun bí Jooseefi yii: «Ma mun dámintì yii bikpɔnɔmɔntiibi tikabiti tita ŋɔ́kɔ́ tɔb pu ní m tú.
translated: I saw Joseph's brothers selling him to the Ishmaelites, and they took him to Egypt. Now Joseph's brothers said to him, "You are going to be sold to the Ishmaelites and you will be taken to Egypt."

ref: Abram asked, “Lord God, how can I know the land will be mine?”
text: Ní Abram bí yii: «Ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti, m làá ŋá mana kí béé yii man di làá nīn lafun yì kitiŋ gbanti?»
translated: Abram said to him: "Lord God, what will you give me, since I am childless and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?"

ref: After Joseph was born, Jacob said to Laban, “Release me from our agreement and let me return to my own country.
text: Raseel nín màl̄ Jooseefi boonee ní Jaakɔb bí Laaban yii: «Pɔ̄-mi nsan ḿ fàtīí nīn kùǹ m-tiŋkin.
translated: “Give me a blessing, my father, before you die.”
